skill.name: writing-beats
skill.description: Shape an article as a journey of beats, choose-your-own-adventure style. The user picks a starting beat from the raw material, you write only that beat, then offer options for where to pivot next, beat by beat, until the article reaches a natural end. Use when the user has raw material and wants to assemble it as a narrative rather than an argument.
skill.summary: Build articles one narrative beat at a time from raw material.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static warnings at SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:10 are false positives: the text describes article structure and asks for an output path. The skill does direct user-approved file reads and writes for drafting, so the remaining risk is limited to filesystem changes.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is the skill description. It uses narrative writing language and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security-sensitive operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter prose about article structure. No evidence of cryptographic code or weak algorithm usage is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line asks where to save the article if no path was provided. It does not request system discovery, environment details, or host enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is limited to asking for a user-selected save path. It does not instruct listing files, probing directories, or collecting system information.

low_findings.title: User-Directed File Editing
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to append, reread, and edit an article file. This is expected behavior, but users should choose the output path carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file editing behavior is explicit and central to the skill. It appears user-directed and non-malicious, so the risk remains low.

content.value_statement: Long drafts can lose shape when all ideas are arranged at once. This skill turns raw material into an article through user-chosen narrative beats.
actual_capabilities: Proposes several starting beats from supplied raw material.
Writes only the beat selected by the user.
Offers next-beat options after each written section.
Rereads the article file before each new write.
Preserves user edits when continuing the draft.
Supports rewrites or replacement of earlier beats.
limitations: Requires raw material from the user before drafting.
Needs a user-approved article file path for saving work.
Does not generate a full article in one pass.
Depends on user choices to decide the article direction.

use_cases.title: Shape an Essay From Notes
use_cases.description: Turn scattered research notes into a guided sequence of article sections.

use_cases.title: Develop a Blog Draft
use_cases.description: Build a post gradually while choosing each pivot and transition.

use_cases.title: Refine Editorial Flow
use_cases.description: Test alternate openings and next sections before committing to a draft.

prompt_templates.title: Start From Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-beats with my raw notes. Suggest three starting beats, then wait for my choice before writing.

prompt_templates.title: Continue a Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Resume work from an existing article file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-beats on this existing draft and raw material. Reread the draft, then suggest the next three beats.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Alternate Openings
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare different entry points before drafting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-beats to propose openings with different tones. Show where each opening could lead before I choose.

prompt_templates.title: Restructure Mid-Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced revision after several beats are written.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-beats to revisit beat 3. Offer replacements, preserve later user edits, and continue from the revised flow.

output_examples.input: Raw notes about a product launch, customer hesitation, and a surprising support insight.
output: Starting beat: Open with the support ticket that revealed the real customer fear.
Alternate beat: Start with the launch metric that looked good until the team read the comments.
Alternate beat: Begin with the old assumption the launch disproved.

output_examples.input: A draft already has an opening about a failed experiment.
output: Next beat: Show the small detail that made the failure useful.
Next beat: Pivot to the first person who noticed the pattern.
Next beat: Zoom out to the decision the team had to make next.

best_practices: Provide raw material with enough notes, quotes, and examples.
Choose a save path before writing starts.
Review each beat before asking for the next options.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a complete article in one pass.
Do not mix unrelated raw material without a clear article goal.
Do not skip rereading the file after manual edits.

faq.question: What is a writing beat?
faq.answer: A beat is one focused move in an article, such as a scene, question, example, or turn.

faq.question: Does this skill write the whole article at once?
faq.answer: No. It writes one selected beat at a time and waits before continuing.

faq.question: Can I change an earlier beat?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports rewriting or replacing a previous beat before continuing.

faq.question: Does it preserve my edits?
faq.answer: Yes. It rereads the article file before each write and treats user edits as authoritative.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Raw notes, excerpts, examples, quotes, and rough ideas work well.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
